name: NIST Building 7 Report
description: The NIST Building 7 Report refers to the National Institute of Standards and Technology's (NIST) investigation into the collapse of 7 World Trade Center (WTC 7) on September 11, 2001. WTC 7, a 47-story skyscraper, collapsed approximately 7 hours after the collapse of the Twin Towers. The NIST report concluded that the collapse was primarily caused by uncontrolled fires resulting from debris from the collapse of the Twin Towers, which weakened critical structural supports. Conspiracy theories surrounding the report allege that the collapse was a controlled demolition, citing the speed and manner of the building's descent as evidence. Proponents often point to the alleged lack of visible fire damage and the symmetrical nature of the collapse. Mainstream scientific consensus, supported by NIST's findings and independent analyses, maintains that the collapse was a natural consequence of fire-induced structural failure. Critics of the conspiracy theories emphasize the detailed modeling and simulations conducted by NIST, which accounted for the effects of fire and structural damage. The report and surrounding theories have been a source of ongoing debate and scrutiny, fueling broader narratives about government cover-ups and the events of 9/11. The NIST report acknowledges uncertainties in their model but asserts that the overall conclusions are robust and supported by the available evidence. Alternative theories often focus on the use of explosives, though no physical evidence has ever been presented to support this claim. The official report details the progression of structural failures from fire damage and explains the observed collapse sequence.
subtitle: Controversy surrounding WTC 7's collapse.

claims.claim: WTC 7 was brought down by controlled demolition.
claims.evidence: The speed and symmetrical nature of the collapse resemble controlled demolitions.
claims.criticism: NIST's report details fire-induced structural failure as the primary cause, explaining the collapse sequence without explosives. The symmetrical appearance is attributed to the building's structural design and the pattern of failures.

claims.claim: The fires in WTC 7 were not hot enough or widespread enough to cause a complete collapse.
claims.evidence: Photographic and video evidence show limited visible fire damage.
claims.criticism: NIST's simulations demonstrated that even localized fires could cause critical structural elements to fail, leading to a progressive collapse. Visible fire damage doesn't necessarily reflect internal temperatures and structural weakening.

claims.claim: NIST's report is a cover-up to conceal the true cause of the collapse.
claims.evidence: Allegations of political pressure and conflicts of interest within NIST.
claims.criticism: NIST's investigation involved extensive peer review and independent analysis. No credible evidence of manipulation or suppression of data has been presented.

keyFigures.name: Shyam Sunder
keyFigures.role: Lead investigator of the NIST WTC 7 report.

keyFigures.name: David Chandler
keyFigures.role: Prominent proponent of the controlled demolition theory.

timeline.event: NIST releases its final report on WTC 7.
Continued debate and scrutiny of the NIST report.

sources.author: NIST
sources.work: Final Report on the Collapse of World Trade Center Building 7
